(("Overweight"[Mesh] OR "Obesity"[Mesh] OR overweight[tiab] OR obese[tiab] OR "excess weight"[tiab]) AND ("Adult"[Mesh] OR adult*[tiab]) AND ("Weight Loss"[Mesh] OR "Weight Reduction Programs"[Mesh] OR "weight management"[tiab] OR "weight loss"[tiab] OR "weight control"[tiab]) AND ("Telemedicine"[Mesh] OR telemedicine[tiab] OR telehealth[tiab] OR telecare[tiab] OR videoconferenc*[tiab] OR "video consultation*"[tiab] OR "video visit*"[tiab] OR telephone[tiab] OR "phone call*"[tiab] OR telecoach*[tiab] OR telecounsel*[tiab] OR "live chat"[tiab] OR synchronous[tiab] OR "real-time"[tiab]) AND (barrier*[tiab] OR facilitat*[tiab] OR enabler*[tiab] OR determinant*[tiab] OR acceptab*[tiab] OR feasib*[tiab] OR "patient perspective*"[tiab] OR "user experience*"[tiab] OR "patient experience*"[tiab] OR motivat*[tiab] OR capability[tiab] OR opportunity[tiab] OR "COM-B"[tiab] OR COMB[tiab]) AND ("Qualitative Research"[Mesh] OR qualitative[tiab] OR interview*[tiab] OR "focus group*"[tiab] OR "thematic analys*"[tiab] OR "framework analys*"[tiab] OR "content analys*"[tiab] OR "grounded theory"[tiab] OR "mixed methods"[tiab] OR "mixed-method*"[tiab]))

inclusion_criteria: Adults (≥18 years) with BMI ≥27.5 kg/m² participating in a health intervention for weight management.
Intervention includes synchronous (real-time), remote delivery with interactive contact (e.g., phone/video/live chat), focused on weight management.
Reports empirical data on barriers and/or facilitators to engagement with the intervention, suitable for mapping to capability, opportunity, or motivation (COM-B) domains.
Primary research using qualitative, quantitative, or mixed-methods designs.
exclusion_criteria: Wrong population: participants <18 years or BMI <27.5 kg/m²; mixed samples without separable data meeting BMI/age criteria.
Wrong intervention: asynchronous-only or fully autonomous digital tools; exclusively face-to-face delivery; non-weight-management or non-health programs.
Wrong outcomes: studies reporting only predictors/usage metrics without explicit barriers/facilitators to engagement; no engagement-related data.
Wrong study type: reviews, protocols, editorials, conference abstracts, and case reports/series (non-empirical).
screening_criteria.description: Apply to identify primary empirical studies on adults with BMI ≥27.5 receiving synchronous, remote weight management interventions that report barriers/facilitators to engagement (mappable to COM-B).

included_articles.title: Participant perspectives of a telehealth trial investigating the use of telephone and text message support in obesity management: a qualitative evaluation.
